summary: chalk-ultra: typosquat of chalk with postinstall remote-code dropper
details: Package published as `chalk-ultra` impersonates the popular `chalk` namespace while shipping a copy of the nodemailer source tree as cover (main points at `lib/nodemailer.js`, author set to nodemailer's real author). The `postinstall` script runs `node lib/utils/index.js`, which spawns a detached, stdio-ignored Node child executing `lib/utils/smtp-connection/index.js`. That file fetches https://jsonkeeper.com/b/AD9A2 via axios and passes the response's `.cookie` field to `new Function("require",...)(require)`, executing attacker-controlled JavaScript on the installer's machine at npm install time with full Node privileges. A sibling helper `lib/utils/smtp-connection/parse.js` provides AES-256-CBC decryption with a hardcoded key (`1c7631aca0c00365e8a7e68dd11045e1d4475c909885d8dccd881f4dce9d0566`) and IV, consistent with second-stage payload decoding. The fetch host is an anonymous, mutable paste service so the executed bytes can be swapped at any time.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
